L1.area: Cryptographic content encoding and message processing wrappers

L2.description: A use-after-free vulnerability exists where a temporary memory buffer object is created and immediately freed during a cryptographic content creation operation, but a raw pointer to the freed memory is still used in a subsequent foreign function interface call. An attacker could exploit this to cause memory corruption, potentially leading to arbitrary code execution.

L3.area: CMS (Cryptographic Message Syntax) content creation and signing operations
L3.description: A use-after-free vulnerability exists in CMS (Cryptographic Message Syntax) content creation where a temporary memory buffer holding optional input data is created and immediately freed, but a raw pointer to the freed memory is still passed to the underlying C library's content signing call. An attacker could exploit this to cause memory corruption, potentially leading to arbitrary code execution.

ground_truth.reason: The CmsContentInfo::sign function creates a MemBioSlice temporary that is immediately dropped after calling as_ptr(), leaving a dangling pointer that is passed to the ffi::CMS_sign call, resulting in a use-after-free of the BIO memory buffer.
